summary: Matched and typed several gmmain_lib functions around game-mode state accessors, render-mode selection, reset/startup helpers, and time delta tracking. The PR replaced multiple placeholder stubs and commented-out implementations with real C, refined struct fields in gm/types.h, corrected function prototypes across gm_1601.h, gmmain_lib.h, and toy.h, and adjusted include boundaries so users of gmmain_lib can forward-declare gm types while implementation/static headers include full type definitions.
what_changed: Added real C implementations for previously unmatched gmmain_lib routines including gmMainLib_8015F588, gmMainLib_8015FA34, gmMainLib_8015FB68, and gmMainLib_8015FC74.

Moved and enabled previously commented-out accessor/flag functions such as gmMainLib_8015ED98, gmMainLib_8015EDB0, gmMainLib_8015EDBC, gmMainLib_8015EDC8, gmMainLib_8015EDD4, gmMainLib_8015EE0C, gmMainLib_8015EE44, and gmMainLib_8015EE90.
Implemented gmMainLib_8015EE68 to clear bit 2 in thing.unk_4 and reset thing.x1CB0.unk from gmMainLib_803D4A60[6].
Introduced the data object gmMainLib_803D4A60 as an eight-int initializer used by the matched code.
Changed gmMainLib_8015CC40 from returning void* to returning struct gmm_x1868*, and adjusted callers to route through gmMainLib_8015CC40 where useful.

Refactored render-mode selection in gmMainLib_8015F500 and implemented gmMainLib_8015F588 using gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x8 as the progressive/interlaced selector and choosing among gmMainLib_803D4A80, GXNtsc480Prog, GXNtsc480IntDf, and GXNtsc480Int before calling HSD_VISetConfigure.
Changed gmMainLib_8015FA34 to take an int argument and updated gm_1AED.c so gm_801AF568_OnFrame passes temp_r29 from lb_8001CBBC into it.
Corrected prototypes: gm_80164F18 is now void gm_80164F18(void), gmMainLib_8015F588 is void(bool), gmMainLib_8015FA34 is void(int), gmMainLib_8015FC74 returns int, and un_80311960 is void(void).
Updated struct gmm_x1CB0 by replacing part of padding after saved_language with an int unk and a one-byte padding field, enabling field-based code instead of raw padding indexing.
Updated struct gmMainLib_8046B0F0_t so x8 is a bool with comment indicating true means progressive and false means interlaced.

Adjusted includes so gmmain_lib.h uses placeholder.h and melee/gm/forward.h instead of pulling in gm/types.h directly, while gmmain_lib.static.h and consumer C files include gm/types.h where full definitions are needed.
smart_moves: Converted raw padding accesses like padding_x1868 offsets into named struct fields such as thing.unk_8, thing.unk_28, thing.unk_30, thing.unk_1A8, and thing.unk_4, making small accessor matches clearer and reusable.
Used a typed accessor return for gmMainLib_8015CC40, which reduced repeated long global dereferences and allowed later code to access x1CB0 through a consistent base pointer.
Kept public header dependencies lighter by switching gmmain_lib.h from full gm/types.h inclusion to forward declarations, then explicitly adding gm/types.h in files that need complete structs.
Factored gmMainLib_8015F4E8 into gmMainLib_8015F500 for the x10[5] render setting, matching behavior while centralizing access to that byte.
Documented gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x8 as the progressive/interlaced flag, turning an integer field into a meaningful bool that explains the render-mode branches.
Matched gmMainLib_8015FA34 by preserving the loop/control-flow shape over player indices 1 through 8 and the debug-level path that resets flags and calls several gm routines before audio/video setup.
decomp_lessons: Small accessor functions can often be unlocked by refining the containing struct layout; replacing padding with minimally named fields is enough to turn commented-out raw-offset code into matching source.
Prototype corrections should propagate to call sites immediately. Here, changing gmMainLib_8015FA34 from unknown parameters to int required updating gm_801AF568_OnFrame to pass temp_r29.
Forward declarations in public headers help avoid broad type coupling, but any C file that dereferences the forwarded structs must include the full types header explicitly.
Boolean state fields should be typed as bool when branch semantics are known; this can improve both readability and matching for conditionals like progressive versus interlaced VI configuration.
When matching render configuration selection, preserving an explicit temporary-pointer branch structure can matter; the code uses var_r0/var_r3 style temporaries before HSD_VISetConfigure.
A TODO in gmMainLib_8015FA34 notes that the call to gmMainLib_8015CC40 should not be inlined, suggesting future matching work may need to watch inlining/codegen around this function.
naming_conventions: Unidentified game-mode library symbols retain address-based names such as gmMainLib_8015FA34 and gmMainLib_803D4A60.
Partially understood struct fields use offset-style or generic names such as x1CB0, x10, unk_4, unk_8, unk_28, unk_30, and unk_1A8.
Return helper structs are named by the accessor address pattern, for example struct gmm_retval_ED98, struct gmm_retval_EDB0, and struct gmm_retval_EDBC.
The global runtime state struct retains an address-based type name gmMainLib_8046B0F0_t, with field comments used to capture known semantics.
Public prototypes use exact return and parameter types once known, replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS when evidence supports it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced placeholder stubs with C implementations while keeping branch-heavy code close to expected compiler shape, especially in gmMainLib_8015F500 and gmMainLib_8015F588.
Used typed pointers and accessor chaining, for example gmMainLib_8015CC40()->x1CB0, to influence emitted address calculations.
Preserved explicit local temporaries such as temp_r30 in gmMainLib_8015FC74 and var_r0/var_r3 in render-mode functions, likely to match register allocation/control flow.
Changed tmp->x8 assignment in gmMainLib_8015FCC0 from 0 to false after typing gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x8 as bool.
Introduced gmMainLib_803D4A60 with exact integer initializer values so matched code can reference global data rather than hard-coded or missing storage.
Left a matching note in gmMainLib_8015FA34: the call to gmMainLib_8015CC40 should not be inlined, indicating a known codegen sensitivity.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human text excerpt is empty, so no specific feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Inspect future or neighboring PRs for the remaining unmatched gmmain_lib functions gmMainLib_8015F600 and any codegen issue around gmMainLib_8015FA34 inlining gmMainLib_8015CC40.
Search for later renames or field refinements of struct gmm_x1868, struct gmm_x1CB0.unk, and gmMainLib_803D4A60.
Check whether gmMainLib_8015FB68 later receives a precise prototype in gmmain_lib.h instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Look for additional render-mode matching work involving gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x8, GXNtsc480Prog, and GXNtsc480IntDf.
